git_history.message: Phase 0: native tool framework upgrade + gap tools + 135 typecheck fixes
fix(core): architecture hardening — cache atomicity, coordination bugs, lifecycle gating, placeholder tools, reporting audit
WIP on dev: 4ce723230 Fix desktop app behavior across core flows

bug_report.summary: Tool cache inflight dedup non-atomic — Ref.get+mutate allows duplicate concurrent computation on same key

race_condition.scenario: Two concurrent fibers for the same cache key both calling getOrCompute simultaneously
race_condition.fiber_a: Ref.get(inflight) → gets Map M → pending.get(key) → undefined
race_condition.fiber_b: Ref.get(inflight) → gets Map M (same reference) → pending.get(key) → undefined
race_condition.overlap: Both read 'no inflight entry' before either writes. Both fibers see no existing Deferred.
race_condition.result: Fiber A: pending.set(key, dA) → Fiber B: pending.set(key, dB) (overwrites A's!) → BOTH start compute()
race_condition.root_cause: Ref.get + mutation is non-atomic (TOCTOU race). Ref.get returns the same Map reference, but there's no locking between the read and the write.

the_fix.before: const pending = yield* Ref.get(inflight); const existing = pending.get(key); ... pending.set(key, d)
the_fix.after: const existing = yield* Ref.modify(inflight, (pending) => { const existing = pending.get(key); if (existing) return [existing, pending] as const; pending.set(key, d); return [undefined, pending] as const })
the_fix.why_it_works: Ref.modify runs the callback atomically — the check (pending.get) and insert (pending.set) happen under a lock. No interleaving possible.

cache_hit_promotion_non_atomic.issue: Ref.get(cache) + direct map.delete(key)/map.set(key, entry) without Ref.update. The hit promotion mutates the Map reference directly, but between Ref.get and the mutation, another fiber's Ref.update(cache, ...) (line ~144) could replace the stored Map. Mutations on the old Map are lost.
cache_hit_promotion_non_atomic.impact: Lost LRU promotion (performance, not correctness). Stale entry may be evicted prematurely.
cache_hit_promotion_non_atomic.pre_existing_finding: ses_1878690c — ToolCache.inflight map mutated via direct reference mutation bypassing Ref.update at cache.ts:108

expiry_eviction_non_atomic.issue: Same pattern — Ref.get(cache) + direct map.delete(key) for expired entries
expiry_eviction_non_atomic.impact: Expired entry may not be removed from the canonical cache Map if another fiber replaced it via Ref.update concurrently.

inflight_cleanup_mutates_in_place.issue: Ref.update(inflight, (m) => { m.delete(key); return m }) — mutates Map and returns same reference. While atomic (inside Ref.update), idiomatically should create a new Map.
inflight_cleanup_mutates_in_place.impact: Style debt only. Safe in practice due to Ref.update's atomicity.

getOrCompute_dead_api.issue: ToolCache.getOrCompute is defined but never called anywhere in the codebase (pre-existing finding from ses_1878690c)

ref_patterns.atomic_rmw: Ref.modify — used correctly for inflight dedup at cache.ts:101
ref_patterns.atomic_effectful: SynchronizedRef.modifyEffect — used in effect/runner.ts, background/job.ts, acp-next/directory.ts
ref_patterns.non_atomic_read_write: Ref.get + separate Ref.update — cache.ts:77→144 (benign due to inflight guard)
ref_patterns.atomic_swap: Ref.getAndSet — used in flight-recorder.ts for buffer drain

canonical_idiom.modify: Ref.modify(ref, (state): readonly [Return, typeof state] => { return [result, newState] as const })
canonical_idiom.clone_before_mutate: new Map(state).set(key, value) — used in acp-next/session.ts:119
canonical_idiom.not: mutating the Map reference obtained from Ref.get without creating a new Map

pre_existing_findings_applied.finding: ToolCache.inflight map mutated via direct reference mutation bypassing Ref.update
pre_existing_findings_applied.current_relevance: Partly addressed by commit 61a7810d8 (inflight dedup now uses Ref.modify). Cleanup code at lines 122-126 still mutates in place inside Ref.update (atomic but not idiomatic).

pre_existing_findings_applied.finding: ToolCache.getOrCompute is defined but never called — dead API surface
pre_existing_findings_applied.current_relevance: Still true — getOrCompute on the Interface is what the layer implements, but the Interface itself may only be consumed indirectly via the Service tag.
